content:
CRITICAL FIRST STEP:
1. Read the LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT included in this prompt. Treat it as the authoritative assignment for this round.
2. If you need the stored context verbatim or want to inspect changes, use context_read(action="read", name="Delegation Instructions") before proceeding.
3. Do NOT proceed based on memory from earlier rounds when the live snapshot is present.

GENERATION MODE:
IMPORTANT: The ontology has exactly ONE namespace: <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>. Type every new instance's rdf:type with an IRI in this EXACT namespace (e.g. <https://example.org/ns/riepr#YourClass>, or declare `@prefix : <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>` yourself and use `:YourClass`). Do NOT bind `:`/`ex:` or any other prefix to a different namespace, and do NOT invent any other namespace either - not a generic placeholder, not a variant of this one, not a per-topic or per-class namespace of your own. Every class lives in the ONE namespace above, with no exceptions.
RICHNESS PROFILE: RICH
- Generate required SHACL triples and substantial realistic optional enrichment (context, relationships, provenance, temporal details).
- You may invent new predicates for useful domain facts when existing ontology predicates are insufficient. Keep them consistent and reusable.

- If NO instances of your assigned target class exist, CREATE them from scratch.
- Generate realistic instances that satisfy SHACL constraints.
- Type every new instance with the EXACT target class URI from the SHACL/Ontology context (the
  same namespace as the shapes' sh:targetClass). Do NOT invent a different or placeholder
  namespace, even one that looks plausible or related — an instance typed in the wrong namespace
  will not match any shape, so it will never actually be validated.
- A shape's own name is the name of a validation rule, not necessarily a class - never assume it
  is one. Always look up your assigned shape's sh:targetClass property in the SHACL context and
  use THAT value's exact URI as the rdf:type for new instances.
- Reuse existing ontology/vocabulary predicates; do not invent new ones (see delegated shape's
  sh:path values and the ontology context for what already exists).
- Look up your assigned target class's `hydra:search [ a hydra:IriTemplate ; hydra:template "..." ;
  hydra:mapping [...] ]` in the Ontology context and mint new instance IRIs by substituting real
  values into that exact template. Only fall back to another URI pattern/convention if the class
  has no hydra:search template. A resolved template contains `/` in its path, so write it as a
  full IRI in angle brackets (`<...>`) - NEVER as a `prefix:LocalName` prefixed name, since a
  prefixed name's local part cannot contain an unescaped `/` and will fail to parse.
- Create required supporting resources when needed.
- Mint a named IRI for every instance you create. Do NOT use blank nodes (`[ ... ]` or `_:id`) for primary domain entities; reserve them for genuinely anonymous auxiliary structures only (e.g. RDF list cells).
- Do NOT include `#` comments in the Turtle you write (no worker/pool/range bookkeeping notes) — only RDF triples.

QUALITY-FIRST RULES:
- Work ONLY on your assigned target class from delegation context.
- Make minimum changes required for conformance.
- Do not modify other workers' target classes.
- Do NOT edit unrelated resources just to reduce global violation count.

VALIDATION LOOP:
- After changes, run shacl_validator(source="store").
- Fix only violations tied to your assigned shape(s)/focus nodes.
- If unrelated violations remain, report and stop.
- If there are no violations for your assigned shape/focus nodes in this pass, do not write triples and report VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE.
- If shacl_validator reports unrecognized predicates/classes on subjects you just created, remove
  those specific triples with triplestore_remove and re-add them using only predicates/classes
  from the delegated shape's sh:path values or the ontology context. Do not report BLOCKED for
  vocabulary you invented yourself - fix it and re-validate instead.

CHANGE SAFETY:
- Prefer additive fixes over destructive removals.
- Remove triples only when required to resolve a specific violation.
- Keep URI/prefix usage consistent with existing data.



DONE CRITERIA:
- At least one valid instance of your assigned target class exists (or more if cardinality requires).
- Assigned shape(s) have no remaining violations.
- Required properties/cardinalities are satisfied.

MANDATORY REPORTING (TOOL, NOT FREE TEXT):
- You MUST call:
  worker_progress(
    status=<CREATED|FIXED|VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE|BLOCKED>,
    target_shape=<shape name>,
    target_class=<class>,
    changed_triples_count=<integer>,
    created_or_updated_subjects=<comma-separated URIs or NONE>,
    validation_result=<CONFORMS|NON_CONFORMS>,
    remaining_issues=<short text or NONE>
  )
- Then provide a brief human summary (1-3 lines) consistent with the tool payload.


Assignment context: shapes 0-1 (worker 1 of 4). Follow your delegated task exactly (generate or fix as instructed). Use triplestore_add/remove/read and validation tools as needed.

LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT (authoritative for this round; use this even if you do not call context_read):
TASK: Process InstallationShape (class :Installation) and EmissionPointShape (class :EmissionPoint). CREATE new instances for both classes.

IRI PATTERNS (from hydra:search):
- Installation: <https://example.org/id/Installation/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}> a :Installation ; ...
- EmissionPoint: <https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}> a :EmissionPoint ; ...

SUBSTITUTE real values for {uuid} (localId), {issued} (xsd:date), {created} (xsd:dateTime). Use xsd:dateTime format "2024-01-15T10:00:00" for created and xsd:date format "2024-01-15" for issued.

MANDATORY SHACL CONSTRAINTS (minCount=1):
Both classes require: rdfs:label, dc:issued, dc:created, dc:modified, adms:status, sosa:isHostedBy (:OperationLocation)

OPTIONAL: ogc:hasGeometry, dc:type, dc:valid, ssn:hasSubSystem, prov:wasRevisionOf, adms:identifier, ssn:hasDeployment

EXAMPLE Installation:
<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> a :Installation ;
  rdfs:label "Glass Production Line 1"@en ;
  dc:issued "2024-01-15"^^xsd:date ;
  dc:created "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  dc:modified "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  adms:status :actief ;
  sosa:isHostedBy <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> .

EXAMPLE EmissionPoint (stack type):
<https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/emis-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> a :EmissionPoint ;
  rdfs:label "Stack 1 Glass Factory"@en ;
  dc:issued "2024-01-15"^^xsd:date ;
  dc:created "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  dc:modified "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  adms:status :actief ;
  sosa:isHostedBy <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> ;
  dbo:diameter "1.5"^^xsd:decimal ;
  dbo:height "25"^^xsd:decimal .

PREDICATES MUST come from ontology/SHACL context only - never invent predicates. Each triplestore_add is validated as standalone Turtle.

REPORT: worker_progress(status, target_shape, target_class, changed_triples_count, validation_result, remaining_issues)

If this live snapshot differs from your memory of earlier rounds, follow this snapshot. Use context_read to inspect the stored context verbatim when needed.
context_reader({"action":"read","name":"Delegation Instructions"})
triplestore_read({"mode":"count"})
{"message":"The shared triple store contains 0 triples.","count":0,"status":"success"}

The triple store is empty. I need to create new instances for both :Installation and :EmissionPoint classes. Let me also check the ontology context to see available predicates and ensure I'm using them correctly.
context_reader({"action":"read","name":"Ontology Context"})
{"error":"Context with name 'Ontology Context' not found"}
context_reader({"action":"list"})

{"changed_count":4,"contexts":[{"changed":true,"name":"example file","type":"text/plain","size":613},{"changed":true,"name":"Ontology","type":"text/turtle","size":100053},{"changed":true,"name":"Ontology Summary","type":"text/plain","size":1465},{"changed":true,"name":"Worker Progress Report","type":"text/plain","size":0},{"changed":false,"name":"Delegation Instructions","type":"text/plain","size":2061}],"message":"There are 4 changed context(s) since your last query","count":5}
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":0.0})
Let me continue reading the ontology to find :Installation and :Emissionpunt class definitions and check for hydra:search templates.
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":30000.0})

I found the key information. I can see that:

1. **Installation** has a hydra:search template with URI pattern: `https://example.org/id/Installation/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}`
2. **EmissionPoint** (which is named as `:Emissionpunt` in Dutch) has a hydra:search template with URI pattern: `https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}`

Let me also check for :OperationLocation since I will need to create it as the host location.
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":60000.0})

I now have enough context. Let me create two sets of instances - one Installation and one EmissionPoint, each with their required OperationLocation host. 

Based on the delegation:
- **Installation** URI pattern: `https://example.org/id/Installation/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}`
- **EmissionPoint** URI pattern: `https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}`

I need to create OperationLocation instances first since both classes require `sosa:isHostedBy (:OperationLocation)`.
{"total_triples":52,"triples_added":34,"message":"Added 34 triples. Shared store now contains 52 triples.","status":"success"}
Now let me add the Installation and EmissionPoint instances as required by my task.
{"total_triples":117,"triples_added":31,"message":"Added 31 triples. Shared store now contains 117 triples.","status":"success"}
Now let me validate the triple store with SHACL to check for conformance.
shacl_validator({"source":"store"})

The validation fails for several reasons:

1. **My new instances (install-001, install-002, emis-001, emis-002) are properly typed under :Emissionpunt which is the Dutch name in the ontology.**
   - I need to check: In SHACL context, the target class uses `:Installation` and `:Emissionpunt` (Dutch), not `:EmissionPoint`.

2. There are existing instances in the store that have violations (like :actief status being typed incorrectly). But my task is specifically about InstallationShape and EmissionPointShape classes.

Let me check what target classes are actually used for InstallationShape and EmissionPointShape by reading more context or checking if there's a SHACL-specific context file that shows the shapes.

Looking at the ontology, I see that:
- The class `:Installation` is defined with proper constraints  
- The class is defined as `:Emissionpunt` (Dutch), not `:EmissionPoint`. My task mentioned "EmissionPoint" but it should map to `:Emissionpunt` in this ontology.

The issue is that my new instances use correct URIs and the target classes match the hydra templates. Looking at my data more closely:
- I used `<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/...>` with type `:Installation` ✓
- I used `<https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/emis-001/...>` with type `:Emissionpunt` ✓

The SHACL validation shows class constraint violations for some pre-existing data, but my new instances don't show violations from the report. Let me specifically query what issues remain related to MY newly created subjects only.
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","subject":"https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00"})

{"triples":["<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Installation> .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label> \"Glass Production Line 1\" .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/created> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\" .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/issued> \"2024-01-15\" .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\" .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#status> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief> .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/isHostedBy> <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#VerwerkingsInstallatie> ."],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 8 triples from the shared store.","count":8}

My instances look correct. The validation message mentions unrecognized predicates and classes - but that's about other data already in the store (like pre-existing measurements). Let me specifically check if my Installation and Emissionpunt subjects conform by validating just those. Looking at the SHACL results, I notice there was a violation saying:
`ClassConstraint[<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>]: Expected class :<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status> for <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>`

This suggests that `:actief` is being expected as a typed status but it's not properly typed. Let me check how adms:status violations appear in my new Installation instances. The validation report shows this violation exists, but it should apply to the pre-existing data, not mine since I correctly referenced `:actief`.

Let me read more of the ontology context to understand any other shape targets that apply:
context_reader({"action":"read","name":"InstallationShape Shape Targets"})
{"error":"Context with name 'InstallationShape Shape Targets' not found"}
context_reader({"action":"list"})

{"changed_count":1,"contexts":[{"changed":false,"name":"example file","type":"text/plain","size":613},{"changed":false,"name":"Ontology","type":"text/turtle","size":100053},{"changed":true,"name":"Validation Report","type":"text/plain","size":24408},{"changed":false,"name":"Ontology Summary","type":"text/plain","size":1465},{"changed":false,"name":"Worker Progress Report","type":"text/plain","size":0},{"changed":false,"name":"Delegation Instructions","type":"text/plain","size":2061}],"message":"There are 1 changed context(s) since your last query","count":6}
context_reader({"action":"read","name":"Validation Report"})

I see! The validation report shows that my **Installation install-001 and install-002** have violations because `:actief` is not being recognized as a valid adms:Status. The issue is that the SHACL shapes expect the status values to be instances of `<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>` but I'm using my own local class definitions like `:actief`.

Looking at this more carefully, the validation shows violations for my new Installation instances due to:
- adms:status pointing to `:actief` which is defined as a local skos:Concept / adms:Status under `https://example.org/ns/riepr#` 

But the SHACL constraints say it should be `<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>` - this seems to be about the VALUE being used (the status concept), not my subject class. My Installation subjects are properly typed as `:Installation`.

Wait, looking at the example in the Delegation Instructions again:
```
adms:status :actief ;
```

So the expected pattern uses `:actief` directly. But SHACL seems to expect it to be of type `<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>`. Let me check if there's a mapping or equivalence in the ontology that connects my local `:actief` to adms:Status.

Looking at the earlier Ontology context, I saw:
```
:actief  rdf:type             skos:Concept , adms:Status ;
```

So `:actief` IS typed as `adms:Status`. But then why does the SHACL validate it as incorrect? This may be because of how the SHACL validator checks - it might be expecting a specific external vocabulary or there's an issue with namespace scoping.

Let me check the class constraint that appears in the report more closely. The error says:
`ClassConstraint[<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>]: Expected class :<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status> for <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>`

This suggests `:actief` should be an instance of `adms:Status`. It IS declared as such in the ontology. 

The problem might be that SHACL validation on the store is seeing all data, including pre-existing invalid data (like :Operator-op-001 which lacks locn:address). My task says to focus only on my delegated shape and target class violations.

Let me check whether installing proper type declarations for the local `:actief` status would resolve the issue - or if I should use a different approach. Actually, looking back at the ontology, I did see:
```
:actief  rdf:type             skos:Concept , adms:Status ;
```

This means it IS already typed correctly. The issue might be something else in how SHACL interprets this. 

Let me verify my instances have all the required properties for their target classes per the delegation constraints (minCount=1 each for rdfs:label, dc:issued, dc:created, dc:modified, adms:status, sosa:isHostedBy):
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","predicate":"http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"})

{"triples":["<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Glass-Corp> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/locn#Address> .","<https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/emis-002/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Emissionpunt> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter-new-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#Geometry> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/locn#Address> .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-002/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Installation> .","<https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/emis-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Emissionpunt> .","<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Installation> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#Geometry> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Cement-NV> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-cement-mechelen/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation> .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Process> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/locn#Address> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-op-999> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument-inst-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-op-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> ."],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 18 triples from the shared store.","count":18}

My subjects are correctly typed:
- `install-001` → `:Installation` ✓
- `install-002` → `:Installation` ✓
- `emis-001` → `:Emissionpunt` ✓  
- `emis-002` → `:Emissionpunt` ✓

Looking back at the SHACL violation message, it's complaining about my Installation instances' **adms:status value** (`:actief`) being incorrect. This means something is wrong with how I referenced or defined the status values in my data, OR there's pre-existing invalid data causing cascading validation failures.

Actually wait - looking at the Validation Report more closely, it shows violations for both `install-001` AND `install-002`, as well as for other unrelated subjects like `:MeasurementInstrument-inst-001` and `Filter-new-001`. The task instruction says:
> "Fix only violations tied to your assigned shape/focus nodes."

My delegated shapes are InstallationShape and EmissionPointShape, which target classes :Installation and :Emissionpunt (Dutch). The violation I see for my new subjects is about adms:status value being invalid.

But wait - the pre-existing data already has issues with `:actief` status in other entities. If this was a problem, why weren't they broken before? Let me check if maybe there are specific SHACL shapes that have targets I'm hitting but not fully understanding.

Actually, re-reading more carefully - the task said "CREATE new instances" and to verify against my delegated shapes. Looking at the delegation snapshot, my target classes are explicitly :Installation and :EmissionPoint. But in the ontology, the class is named `:Emissionpunt` (Dutch), not `:EmissionPoint`. I used the correct Dutch name which matches the hydra template.

The issue with adms:status seems to stem from how SHACL interprets values - but this is a pre-existing issue since other subjects in the store also have problems with `:actief`. My task says "If shacl_validator flags 'unrecognized_predicates'/'unrecognized_classes' on subjects YOU just created..."

The adms:status violation isn't about unrecognized predicates or classes for my data - it's about SHACL class constraints checking if `:actief` is a valid instance of `adms:Status`. But in the ontology, I already saw that `:actief` IS typed as `adms:Status`:

```
:actief  rdf:type             skos:Concept , adms:Status ;
```

This should be fine. Let me check if adding proper type assertions to my actual status values would help - but actually the ontology already defines it this way for global use, not per-instance.

Let me check the OperationLocation instances I created and ensure they have all required properties too:
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","subject":"https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00"})

{"triples":["<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label> \"Glass Factory Antwerp\" .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/created> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\" .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/issued> \"2024-01-15\" .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\" .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#hasGeometry> <null> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#wasAttributedTo> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Glass-Corp> ."],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 7 triples from the shared store.","count":7}

Good - all OperationLocation instances have their required properties plus geometry. They are correctly typed as :OperationLocation.

Now let me think about this differently. The SHACL validation says violations exist, but most of them seem to relate to pre-existing data (like `:Operator-op-001`, `:MeasurementInstrument-inst-001`, etc.). According to my task instructions, I should report the status based on whether **my delegated shape** has violations after this round.

My assigned shapes are InstallationShape and EmissionPointShape with target classes :Installation and :Emissionpunt. Let me check if there might be specific violations only for my new instances by looking more carefully at what validation messages mention:

From the Validation Report, I see these related to my subjects:
1. `<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-002/...>` - ClassConstraint on adms:status
2. `<https://example.org/id/Installation/install-001/...>` - ClassConstraint on adms:status

Both say "Expected class :<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status> for <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>". This means SHACL expects `:actief` to be an instance of `adms:Status`, but it appears the check is failing.
